title: Gateway: Yggdrasil daemon integration
description:
## Overview

Run Yggdrasil on gateway nodes to join the global backbone.

## Implementation options

### Option 1: External daemon (RPi/Linux)
- Run stock yggdrasil binary
- Configure via yggdrasil.conf
- Gateway communicates via TUN interface
- Simple, well-tested

### Option 2: Embedded library (ESP32/nRF)
- yggdrasil-go can be compiled as library (maybe)
- Or: port core routing to C
- Complex but self-contained

### Option 3: Yggdrasil-lite
- Implement minimal Yggdrasil client
- Just enough to peer and route
- No full DHT, rely on peers

**Recommendation:**
- RPi gateway: Option 1 (external daemon)
- Embedded gateway: Option 3 (lite client), or proxy via RPi

## Configuration

### Peering
```yaml
Peers:
  - tcp://ygg.mkg20001.io:80
  - tcp://yggpeer.example.com:12345
  # Or private peers only
```

### Private key
Import from LICHEN identity:
```yaml
PrivateKey: <LICHEN Ed25519 private key in Yggdrasil format>
```

### Subnet routing
Advertise mesh prefix:
```yaml
# Gateway announces that it can reach these addresses
# (mesh nodes under this gateway)
```

## Zephyr integration (embedded)

If running on Zephyr (not Linux):
- Need Yggdrasil-lite in C
- Or: use serial/IP bridge to external Yggdrasil
- Evaluate complexity vs just requiring RPi for global backbone

## Systemd service (Linux gateway)

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Yggdrasil for LICHEN gateway
After=network.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/yggdrasil -useconffile /etc/lichen/yggdrasil.conf
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```
close_reason: Gateway Option 1 external yggdrasil (TUN/conf/peering/Ed25519 privkey/systemd/subnet-02xx) noted; embedded lite/proxy noted in config; coap_config.h updated for primary/0200 remove fd12/ULA; routing test+is_local_mesh updated for RPL prefer before TUN w/ independent vector oracle; 3 codereview+verify(pytest/cargo/west) passes. Scoped exactly.
